title: Centrostachys heudelotii
taxonomy: Centrostachys heudelotii is classified as a species of plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Centrostachys, which is a group of flowering plants. This plant is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as Cameroon, the Republic of the Congo, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of Centrostachys heudelotii is based on its physical characteristics, including its leaves and flowers. As a result, it is classified as a unique species within the Acanthaceae family.

intro: Centrostachys heudelotii, also known as the 'bitter leaf', is a plant species native to West and Central Africa, characterized by its bitter and slightly sweet taste. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of malaria. The bioactive constituents of Centrostachys heudelotii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional African medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, while in modern herbalism, it may be found in capsule or tincture form, often used to support digestive health and immune function.
